QF.id: IFSCA Introduction to AML CFT and PF_5

question.text: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) was founded in which year and at whose initiative?

answer.text: 1970, at the initiative of the United States Congress
1989, at the initiative of the G7 Summit in Paris

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** FATF was founded in 1989 at the initiative of the G7 Summit held in Paris. Its original mission was to devise and promote international standards to prevent money laundering. In its first year, FATF issued 40 recommendations to fight money laundering. After 9/11, FATF expanded its mandate to include combating terrorist financing.
**The Trap:** The year 1970 refers to the US Bank Secrecy Act. The year 2001 is when FATF expanded its mandate post-9/11. The year 2010 is when India became a FATF member. These are all important dates but none mark FATF's founding.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Series XXIV also tests FATF founding details. The IFSCA exam places additional emphasis on India's FATF membership (2010) and India's membership in two FSRBs -- Asia Pacific Group (APG) and Eurasian Group (EAG).
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the key FATF timeline: Founded 1989, 40 Recommendations issued in first year, expanded mandate post-9/11, India joined in 2010. IFSCA's AML framework is designed to be fully FATF compliant.
